reason: Adjusting permissions on /etc/shadow to the STIG‑specified mode is a common hardening step. The change removes execute, write, setuid/gid, and sticky bits, leaving the file readable only by root. This does not lock out access and will not break the system when performed correctly.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Incorrect permission may prevent authentication services from reading /etc/shadow; Typo in command could set overly restrictive permissions
